definition: A classification of values and operations.
Basic value type such as integer string Boolean or float.
A type combining multiple fields or values.
A type representing one of several alternatives.
A type formed from products and sums.
A fixed set of named alternatives.
A type that represents a present value or explicit absence.
A type that represents success or failure.
A type parameterized by another type.
Reusable code that works uniformly across types.
Compiler or checker discovery of types from expressions.
Type checking before execution.
Type checking during execution.
Combination of typed and untyped code.
Compatibility by declared name.
Compatibility by required shape.
A type constrained by a predicate.
A type that depends on a value.
A type that tracks computational effects.
Machine-readable structure defining external data.
